title: Podman does not mount `--secret` env variables correctly
body:
### Issue Description

Mounting `--secret "id=aa,type=env,target=AA"` does not work correctly.  The mounted file is empty.
on macOS Ventura installed with `brew install podman`.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Run the following 
   ```shell
   echo -n "FROM alpine\n RUN --mount=type=secret,id=aaa echo PASS=\$(cat /run/secrets/aaa)" | \
   A=MYPASSWORD podman build --secret "id=aaa,type=env,target=A" -f -
   ```

### Describe the results you received

The build prints `PASS=` which means its not correctly writing the password to the file.

```shell
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug
STEP 1/2: FROM alpine
STEP 2/2: RUN --mount=type=secret,id=aaa echo PASS=$(cat /run/secrets/aaa)
PASS=
COMMIT
--> d6c72b09ae86
d6c72b09ae8662b2bfc69d06a6ec5cbea478f6d87e765d4849a44de8101dbef1
```


### Describe the results you expected

The build should print `PASS=MYPASSWORD`.

Additional environment details

### Additional information

comments.body:
I think issue is that `podman remote` does not passes ENV from host to machine on the fly, it only does once when the machine is first started, `podman` needs to curate a method so this can be done via API on every build request.

One thing which needs to be checked is, does buildkit have any API for this ( if yes , is it exposed ) if not then podman is free to do it however it likes without honoring buildkit API.
Ah, did not get that from the doc.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
facing the same issue, any updates?
No one has been working on this that I am aware of.

This works on my Linux box.  But not with --remote.

```
printf "FROM alpine\nRUN --mount=type=secret,id=aaa echo PASS=\$(cat /run/secrets/aaa)" | A=MYPASSWORD podman build --no-cache --secret "id=aaa,type=env,src=A" -f -
STEP 1/2: FROM alpine
STEP 2/2: RUN --mount=type=secret,id=aaa echo PASS=$(cat /run/secrets/aaa)
PASS=MYPASSWORD
COMMIT
--> 545d7c234cfe
545d7c234cfe2cf8fb715a58cc343a7d18b3e9e8442a95aa0a59001bed578527
```

> I think issue is that `podman remote` does not passes ENV from host to machine on the fly, it only does once when the machine is first started, `podman` needs to curate a method so this can be done via API on every build request.
> 
> One thing which needs to be checked is, does buildkit have any API for this ( if yes , is it exposed ) if not then podman is free to do it however it likes without honoring buildkit API.

Is there a workaround that involves destroying and creating the Podman remote machine? I'm using podman on a Mac, so making a VM remote is part of routine operations... Thanks!

It's been two and half years since this bug was reported and it's still not fixed. I just wasted my entire morning trying to get environment variables to work with secrets in podman only to have it fail again and again no matter what I tried. I came here to report the bug and found this issue when I searched. +1 for another impacted user due to this bug.

> Interested in working on it?

@rhatdan Unfortunately, I'm a Python programmer and don't know go. I'm guessing this issue is difficult to fix or else it would have been fixed already, so I'm pretty sure a brand new go programmer isn't going to solve this one. Otherwise I would offer to help.

Just confirming that this limitation makes `podman build --secret "id=aaa,type=env,target=A"` not actually work when using podman on Windows (since that's always in remote mode to talk to the WSL instance).

I'm not using v5.8.x yet, though, and https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27602 suggests that this may be fixed in v5.8.0.
